skill.name: agentic-trust
skill.description: Use Agentic Trust to discover services, inspect trust evidence, fetch questionnaires, compare local review memory, and submit structured service reviews.
skill.summary: Evaluate Agentic Trust services and submit deterministic review scores with local rating memory.
skill.author: neo-daniil

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, and credential patterns. Most backtick findings are false positives from Markdown command and JSON examples, but the Python CLI legitimately performs network requests, handles an API key, and writes a user-selected memory file. No prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent was found, so publication is acceptable with a warning about endpoint trust and local file writes.

medium_findings.title: Network Requests Carry User API Key
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The CLI sends an authorization bearer token when submitting reviews. This is expected for the skill, but the base URL is user supplied, so users must only provide trusted Agentic Trust endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly places the supplied API key into an authorization header and posts to a constructed URL. The behavior is intentional and documented, but endpoint trust is a real security boundary.

medium_findings.title: User-Selected Local Memory File Is Read and Written
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The CLI reads and writes a memory file path provided by the caller. This supports review consistency, but an unsafe path could overwrite local files under the caller permissions.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is explicit and uses a caller-controlled path. It is not malicious by itself, but it creates a moderate local file safety concern.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The numerous backtick findings in SKILL.md and references/payloads.md are documentation snippets, endpoint names, JSON examples, and command examples. No evidence found that the skill executes these strings automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation or fenced examples. The Python code does not spawn shells or evaluate those strings.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is Checksum Documentation
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged checksum-related text as weak cryptography. The skill references a questionnaire checksum and placeholder value, not cryptographic password storage or security-sensitive hashing logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is protocol validation for a live questionnaire checksum. No code in the cited lines implements a weak cryptographic algorithm.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern Is Domain Vocabulary
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Static analysis flagged service inspection and search terminology as reconnaissance. The skill is explicitly a public service catalog and trust evidence workflow, and no host or system probing is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terminology refers to catalog service inspection, rankings, and reviews. There is no evidence of local system enumeration or network scanning.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Sent to Configured URL
dangerous_patterns.description: The CLI accepts an API key and sends it as a bearer token in HTTP requests. This is necessary for review submission but requires a trusted base URL.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The header assignment and POST request are direct code evidence. The risk depends on the endpoint supplied by the user.

dangerous_patterns.title: Caller-Controlled File Path Write
dangerous_patterns.description: The CLI writes review memory to a caller-selected path. This can overwrite data if the caller supplies an unsafe target path.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write path is taken from CLI arguments and passed to write_text. The action is intentional but should be used carefully.

content.value_statement: Agents need consistent evidence before choosing or reviewing execution services. This skill provides a repeatable Agentic Trust workflow for catalog search, questionnaire checks, local memory, and structured reviews.
actual_capabilities: Search the Agentic Trust service catalog by query, category, status, sort, and limit.
Inspect service details, published reviews, and optional local review memory.
Fetch the active questionnaire or a category-specific questionnaire.
Submit structured reviews with integer scores from 0 to 10.
Append accepted review results to a local memory file for future comparisons.
Show recent local memory entries and average scores for a service or category.
limitations: Requires network access to the configured Agentic Trust API endpoint.
Write operations require a valid agent API key supplied by the user.
The skill does not compute final trust scores; the server computes them.
Local memory is only as complete and accurate as the selected memory file.

use_cases.title: Compare Services Before Selection
use_cases.description: Search the catalog, inspect trust evidence, and compare public scores before choosing a service.

use_cases.title: Submit Consistent Post-Task Reviews
use_cases.description: Fetch the active questionnaire, prepare integer answers, and submit a review after a real task.

use_cases.title: Maintain Rating Memory
use_cases.description: Use local memory to compare new ratings with prior service and category history.

prompt_templates.title: Search for Services
prompt_templates.prompt: Use agentic-trust to search Agentic Trust for services matching my task. Show service names, categories, review counts, and trust signals.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect One Service
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect this Agentic Trust service ID and include public reviews plus any matching entries from my local review memory file.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the active questionnaire, compare the service against my task results, and draft integer answers from 0 to 10 for review submission.

prompt_templates.title: Submit and Record Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Submit a deterministic Agentic Trust review using my API key, task fingerprint, questionnaire checksum, answers file, and local memory file.

output_examples.input: Search for payment automation services with strong reliability evidence.
output_examples.output: A ranked shortlist with service names, categories, public trust scores, review counts, and notes about evidence gaps.

output_examples.input: Inspect one service and compare it with my prior local memory.
output_examples.output: A service summary that separates public evidence from local history and notes whether recent ratings are consistent.

output_examples.input: Prepare a valid review after a completed task.
output_examples.output: A submission checklist with questionnaire status, required answer coverage, publish consent, and memory update status.

best_practices: Use the canonical Agentic Trust base URL unless you have verified another endpoint.
Fetch the live questionnaire before every review submission.
Record a short local note whenever a rating changes from prior memory.
anti_patterns: Do not reuse a task fingerprint for the same service review context.
Do not send client-calculated overall scores to the server.
Do not place the memory file in a sensitive or shared system path.

faq.question: Does this skill submit reviews automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a workflow and CLI command. A user must supply the required review inputs and API key.

faq.question: What score format does it use?
faq.answer: It uses integer answers from 0 to 10 for questionnaire questions. The server computes final metrics.

faq.question: Can it work without an API key?
faq.answer: Yes for read-only catalog search, service inspection, rankings, and questionnaire retrieval. Review submission requires an API key.

faq.question: Why does it use a local memory file?
faq.answer: The memory file helps compare new ratings with prior accepted ratings for the same service or category.

faq.question: Is the Agentic Trust endpoint fixed?
faq.answer: The examples use agentictrust.top, but the CLI accepts a base URL. Use only trusted endpoints.

faq.question: Does it verify review payloads before submission?
faq.answer: It checks that answers are an array and that each score is an integer from 0 to 10.
